title: Everything Todds Ever Dreamed
wikitext:
[]: Everything Todd's Ever Dreamed             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon May 27, 2013 2:44 pm http://youtubedoubler.com/8ok9 This is how it could have been. Wait for em both to load, drop the volume on the left one, have a couple of 'Dark Side of Oz' moments (particularly during the second verse), enjoy.

[]:             Quote Post by Paranoid » Mon May 27, 2013 6:15 pm I love Everything You've Ever Dreamed, but this doesn't really work. I can't see any of these Dark Side of Oz moments you speak of (except maybe when it looks like Hyuga and Aoba are singing "love's just a thing that others do", which made me laugh a bit). I wish they included the song in the movie, just not in that scene.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon May 27, 2013 6:41 pm Try this one. I had the EoE clip start a bit earlier, and I think it fits a bit better this way. The problem is that the final scene was probably cut to fit KST. That's why it'll never feel just right. An alternate cut of the scene made to fit EYED would be a great bonus feature for a future EoE 20th Anniversary BD. Is it possible to remove the music track from the scene and leave only the dialogue and sound effects? It would be interesting to see how the whole scene sounds with just the music swapped.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon May 27, 2013 6:48 pm Paranoid wrote: I can't see any of these Dark Side of Oz moments you speak of (except maybe when it looks like Hyuga and Aoba are singing "love's just a thing that others do", which made me laugh a bit).

[]:             Quote Post by Paranoid » Mon May 27, 2013 7:04 pm I really don't feel that they match all that well. The vast majority of those sound like coincidence to me, and I don't feel that they hold any special meaning. Like: 2:52 - "Did she just throw your love away" for the Hyuga/Misato bit (oh come on, thats a doozie)

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Tue May 28, 2013 12:34 am Meh, the song doesn't really go with the scene for me. Maybe it's because I'm a huge Komm Susser Tod fanboy .

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Tue May 28, 2013 1:11 am Paranoid wrote: I just don't feel that it was written with the instrumentality sequence in mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue May 28, 2013 8:14 am Oh blimey, i actually did the youtube doubler thing last time. Sorry for the repeat posting. You can probably go ahead and delete this thread.
